ALL SPECIALTIES / DIETITIAN / PENNSYLVANIA

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Pennsylvania Dietitian License

Pennsylvania Dietitian license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-02-25 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Dietitian applications in Pennsylvania run through Pennsylvania State Board of Nursing. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Official College Transcripts - Must be sent directly from your degree-granting university to the State Board of Nursing.
  • Examination Verification - Verification of a passing score on the CDR or BCNS exam, sent directly from the testing agency.
  • Child Abuse CE Certificate - A certificate proving completion of the mandatory 3-hour Pennsylvania child abuse recognition and reporting course.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 4 to 8 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Pennsylvania does not currently participate in interstate licensure compacts for nutritionists. Out-of-state applicants must apply via endorsement through the PALS system and provide verification of their active, good-standing license from their home state, along with proof of passing a Board-approved exam.

  1. STEP 1

    Create a PALS Account

    Go to the Pennsylvania Licensing System (PALS) at pals.pa.gov and register for a new user account if you do not already have one.

    Fee: $95 · Timeline: 4 to 8 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Submit Online Application

    Log in to your PALS account, click 'Apply for a New License', select 'Medicine' as the board, then choose 'Dietitian-Nutritionist'. Complete the application form and pay the non-refundable application fee of $25.00.

    Fee: $95 · Timeline: 4 to 8 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Arrange for Official Transcripts

    Request your official college or university transcript, verifying a bachelor's or master's degree in human nutrition, food and nutrition, dietetics, or food systems management, to be sent directly to the Pennsylvania State Board of Medicine.

    Fee: $95 · Timeline: 4 to 8 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Submit Supervised Practice Verification

    Ensure verification of your minimum of 900 hours of supervised practice experience is sent directly from your supervisor or program director to the Pennsylvania State Board of Medicine.

    Fee: $95 · Timeline: 4 to 8 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Verify CDR Registration

    Provide a copy of your current registration card from the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) and arrange for CDR to send verification of your Registered Dietitian (RD) status directly to the Board.

    Fee: $95 · Timeline: 4 to 8 Weeks

  6. STEP 6

    Complete Criminal History Record Check

    Follow the instructions provided within the PALS system to complete the required Pennsylvania Criminal History Record Check (CHRC) as part of your application.

    Fee: $95 · Timeline: 4 to 8 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$95
Background check
$0 (included in application)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$95

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
Pennsylvania State Board of Nursing
Phone
(717) 783-1400
Website
dos.pa.gov/ProfessionalLicensing/BoardsCommissions/Nursing
Application page
Open application

Renewal

Dietitian renewal in Pennsylvania follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Biennial (Every 2 years)
Renewal fee
$71
Continuing education
30 hours per renewal cycle

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does it cost to get an LDN license in Pennsylvania?

The initial application fee for a Licensed Dietitian-Nutritionist in Pennsylvania is $95. After issuance, it costs $71 to renew the license biennially.

Which Pennsylvania board issues the dietitian license?

Unlike many states with dedicated dietetics boards, Pennsylvania regulates Licensed Dietitian-Nutritionists under the State Board of Nursing within the broader Department of State.

What are the continuing education requirements for Pennsylvania dietitians?

To renew biennially, you must complete 30 hours of continuing professional education. Of those 30 hours, 2 hours must strictly be a Board-approved course in child abuse recognition and reporting.

Does Pennsylvania have reciprocity for dietitians?

No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Pennsylvania. To work in Pennsylvania, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Pennsylvania?

Most endorsement applications take 4 to 8 Weeks.

Can I start working in Pennsylvania while my endorsement application is processing?

Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$95

Processing time

4 to 8 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Not available

National exam

Registration Examination for Dietitians

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Biennial (Every 2 years) · $71

Licensing board

Pennsylvania State Board of Nursing

dos.pa.gov/ProfessionalLicensing/BoardsCommissions/Nursing

(717) 783-1400

This page is maintained as a reference for dietitians and recruiters evaluating Pennsylvania assignments.
